Danford Fisher Hannig Foundation Main Menu Home About Us Contact Charity Login The Danford Fisher Hannig Foundation is a Redwood City based foundation that supports over 150 local charities. Danford Fisher Hannig Foundation Founded in 1982 by Redwood City residents, Fred and Gladys Danford. Katherine Fisher was their executive assistant, and she assisted the Danfords in putting the Foundation together with the help of their CPA, Dan Shehi, and their attorney, Frank Hannig. The Fisher and Hannig families have donated to and managed the Foundation. The Foundation name was updated as a tribute to Katherine Fisher and Frank Hannig and their generous contribution of a legacy of service and philanthropy that helped build the Foundation since the Danfords’ passing. That legacy continues through Katherine’s son George Fisher and Frank’s son Ted Hannig. Danford Foundation does not maintain offices or staff. Rather, its directors utilize their resources to more efficiently run and manage the Foundation. The Foundation invests in their existing portfolio of 150 charities that support local health, education and welfare. © 2026 Danford Fisher Hannig Foundation Web Design by Wicked Graphics

Employee Contributions We support teammates who give back to the communities. Employee Matching Employees are offered the opportunity to donate to 501(c)(3) charities of their choice each year, and Heffernan provides a dollar-for-dollar match. Employee Volunteer Time All employees receive up to four paid volunteer days each year to support local nonprofits. Heffernan also participates in the Insurance Industry Charitable Foundation’s Annual Month of Giving. Dollars for Doers Employees who volunteer 50 hours or 25 hours at a nonprofit can direct a $500 or $250 grant, respectively, to that organization.

Employees who volunteer 50 hours or 25 hours at a nonprofit can direct a $500 or $250 grant, respectively, to that organization.

                    programs. Animal Support The needs are many when it comes to helping our four (or two!) legged friends. To contact our foundation in the most efficient manner, please complete the application below and submit it. Grant Application Period: March 1 – September 15, 2025 Grant Deadline: September 15, 2025 Due to an overwhelming number of organizations requesting funds, and the use of artificial intelligence to submit unsolicited electronic requests, we no longer accept unsolicited grant applications in 2025. We will only review grant applications if your organization has received a grant from our foundation in the recent past or if you have been expressly invited to submit or renew a grant application by a member of the Grant Committee. We will not read, review, or respond to unsolicited grant applications in 2025. Grant Guidelines: Although any organization in the USA may apply for a grant, our focus is on non-profit organizations that are personally known to one or more of the board members of the foundation. We find a personal connection is often the best way to create a lasting impact. When submitting a grant application please include the following information: Cover Letter telling us about your organization. A copy of your IRS Determination Letter, EIN #, and your last annual report. Answer The Question:  How does your organization make the world better ? Your personal connection to the foundation:  Are you one of the thousands of organizations that has received grants from the us in the past?  Have you recently had an interesting conversation with a board member who suggested you apply for funds? Response Time: In calendar year 2025, we intend to review applications and fund requests in the last quarter of the year (Oct, Nov, Dec).    Please do not contact us for updates.
